Cyber Translations: So is the responsibility to use it safely. Cyber security isn't just for experts — it protects families, communities, and the future of Timor-Leste.
Ida-ne’e mós ita-nia responsabilidade atu uza teknolojia ho seguru. Seguransa sibernétika la’ós de’it ba ema espesialista sira — ida-ne’e proteje família, komunidade, no Timor-Leste nia futuru.

Cyber security is the practice of protecting yourself, your information, and your devices from harm online. It covers everything from keeping passwords strong to recognising when someone is trying to trick or manipulate you.

Seguransa sibernétika mak oinsá ita proteje ita-nia an, ita-nia informasaun, no ita-nia sasán teknolojia sira husi perigu iha mundu dijitál laran. Ida-ne'e inklui buat hotu, hahú husi kria kódigu (password) ne'ebé forte to'o koñese se mak tenta atu lohi ka utiliza ita.
Your name, photo, address, and personal documents are valuable. Cyber security helps keep them out of the wrong hands.
Ita-nia naran, foto, hela-fatin, no dokumentu pesoál sira mak valiozu. Seguransa sibernétika ajuda rai sira hosi liman sala.
Social media, messaging apps, and websites can expose you to scams, harmful content, and people who don't have good intentions.
Rede sosiál, aplikasaun mensajen, no sítiu web sira bele fó risku ba fraude, konteúdu ne’ebé fo prejuízu, no ema sira ne’ebé laiha intensaun di’ak.
Email, banking, school platforms, and government services all need protection — a single weak password can open everything.
Email, banku, plataforma eskola nian, no servisu governu nian, sira hotu presiza protezaun — password ne’ebé fraku ida de’it bele loke buat hotu.
Phones and laptops store enormous amounts of personal data. Knowing how to secure them protects your whole life.
Telemovel no laptop sira rai dadus pesoál ne’ebé barak tebes. Hatene oinsá atu proteje ekipamentu sira-ne’e, katak Ita mós proteje Ita-nia moris tomak.
Digital threats are not abstract — they affect real people every day. As Timor-Leste grows more connected, the risks grow with it.
Ameasa dijitál la'ós buat ne'ebé imajináriu de'it — sira afeta ema loloos loron-loron. Bainhira Timor-Leste sai konektadu liu, risku sira mós boot liu.
Scam messages are sent billions of times every day. They reach every country, every inbox.
Mensajen lasu (scam) billiaun ba billiaun haruka sai loron-loron. Ida-ne'e tama ba nasaun hotu no ema hotu nia inbox
Most successful attacks happen because someone clicked a link, shared a password, or trusted the wrong person.
Atake barak susesu tanba ema klike ba link ruma, fahe sira-nia password, ka fiar ema ne'ebé ladi'ak.
Attacks on smartphones are growing faster than on computers — and mobiles are how most people in Timor-Leste go online.
Atake ba smartphone sira aumenta lalais liu fali kómputer—no ita hatene katak ema barak liu iha Timor-Leste uza telemóvel de'it atu asesu ba internet.
Tamba Saida Mak Ida Ne'e Importante ba Timor-Leste
As one of the world's youngest nations, Timor-Leste is building its digital future right now. That creates real opportunity — and real vulnerability.
Nu’udar nasaun ida ne’ebé foin foin sa’e iha mundu, Timor-Leste harii daudaun ninia futuru dijitál agora. Ida-ne’e kria oportunidade ne’ebé di’ak—maibé mós kria kbiit-fraku ne’ebé reál.
Mobile internet adoption is rising fast, bringing millions online for the first time with little prior digital safety education.

Uza internet iha telemóvel aumenta lalais tebes, ida-ne'e lori ema milliaun ba milliaun atu asesu ba mundu dijitál ba dala uluk, maibé barak mak seidauk iha koñesimentu kona-ba oinsá atu uza internet ho seguru.
Government services, banking, and business are increasingly digital — making secure practices essential for economic participation.

Servisu Governu nian, banku, no negósiu sira agora uza ona sistema dijitál—tanba ne'e, importante tebes ba ita atu aprende oinsá uza teknolojia ho seguru hodi bele partisipa iha dezenvolvimentu ekonómiku.
Young people make up the majority of the population. Their digital habits now will shape the nation's cyber culture for a generation.
Ema foin-sa'e sira mak sai maioria iha ita-nia populasaun. Sira-nia lala'ok iha mundu dijitál ohin loron nian sei fó influénsia ba Timor-Leste nia kultura teknolojia ba jerasaun foun tuir mai.
Small nations can be targeted precisely because they have fewer dedicated cyber security resources and less public awareness.
Nasaun ki’ik sira bele sai targetu tanba de’it sira iha rekursu ba seguransa sibernétika ne’ebé uitoan no mós konsiénsia públiku nian ne’ebé sei menus.
Disinformation and manipulation campaigns can spread rapidly in close-knit communities and small information ecosystems.
Kampaña dezinformasaun no manipulasaun sira bele espalla ho lalais iha komunidade ne’ebé besik malu no iha ekosistema informasaun ne’ebé ki’ik.
A single major breach of a critical system — health, finance, or infrastructure — would have outsized impact on a small population.
Atake boot ida de’it ba sistema ne’ebé importante—hanesan saúde, finansa, ka infrastruktura—sei fó impaktu ne’ebé boot tebes ba ita-nia populasaun ne’ebé ki’ik.
Cyber safety is not just a personal choice. In Timor-Leste, it is a community responsibility and a form of national resilience.
Seguransa dijitál la’ós de’it hili-ba-an-rasik. Iha Timor-Leste, ida-ne’e mak responsabilidade komunidade nian no sai mós forsa ida atu proteje ita-nia nasaun.
When cyber security is ignored, the damage is real — to people, families, institutions, and the nation.
Bainhira ita la fó atensaun ba Seguransa sibernétika, ninia rezultadu ladi'ak ne'e reál tebes—ba ita-nia an, família, instituisaun sira, no ba nasaun tomak.
Personal documents, bank accounts, and online identities stolen and misused — sometimes for years before the victim notices.
Dokumentu pesoál, konta banku, no identidade online sira ema na'ok no uza sala—dala ruma durante tinan barak mak vítima foin hatene.
Scams, fake job offers, and banking fraud drain savings. For low-income households, a single attack can be catastrophic.

Lasu (scam), oferese serbisu falsu, no lohi-teen banku nian bele hamoos ita-nia osan ne'ebé rai hela. Ba família sira ne'ebé kbiit-laek, atake ida de'it bele lori sira ba situasaun ne'ebé susar tebes.
Private images or messages weaponised to extort, humiliate, or control victims — often targeting young people.
Imajen ka mensajen privadu sira uza hanesan kilat atu kobra osan, hamoe, ka kontrola vítima sira—baibain sira mak foka liu ba foin-sa’e sira.
False health, political, or safety information spreads through communities, eroding trust and causing harm.
Informasaun falsu kona-ba saúde, polítika, ka seguransa naklekar iha komunidade laran, ida-ne'e harahun konfiansa no lori mós perigu ba ita hotu.
Schools, hospitals, and government offices hit by attacks lose data and operational capacity, affecting the whole community.
Eskola, ospitál, no eskritóriu governu ne'ebé hetan atake bele lakon sira-nia dadus no mós kapasidade operasionál, ida-ne'e fó impaktu ba komunidade tomak.
Once people distrust digital systems, they avoid them — slowing economic development and access to essential services.
Bainhira ema la fiar ona ba sistema dijitál sira, sira sei hadook an husi ida-ne'e—ida-ne'e sei hametin dezenvolvimentu ekonómiku no hanetik ita-nia asesu ba servisu sira ne'ebé importante.
Cyber safety looks different at every stage of life. Choose the guide that fits you.
Seguransa online haree diferente iha kada etapa moris. Hili guia ne'ebé tuir ho ita.
Potential issue in source: Tetun title appears corrupted as 'Eksplorادór Ki'ik'.
Fun, interactive adventures that teach children the basics of staying safe online — through games, characters, and simple rules.
Aventura divertidu no interativu ne'ebé hanorin fundamentu ba labarik sira atu nafatin seguru online — liu husi jogu, karakter, no regra simples.
Tetun title left in English in source.
Practical skills for social media, gaming, study, and early work life — spotting scams, locking accounts, and protecting your privacy.
Kbiit prátiku ba rede sosiál, jogu, estudu, no servisu — identifika fraude, proteje konta, no kuidadu privasidade.
Guidance for parents, professionals, and community members — protecting family, work, finances, and the people around you.
Orientasaun ba inan-aman, profisionál, no membru komunidade — proteje família, servisu, finansa, no ema sira iha ita-nia sorin.
Individual awareness matters — but real resilience comes when whole families, schools, and communities practise safe habits together.
Konsiensia individuál importante — maibé forsa ne'ebé loos mosu bainhira família, eskola, no komunidade hotu prátika hábitu seguru hamutuk.
Share what you learn with your family, especially older relatives who may be less familiar with digital threats.
Fahe saida mak ita aprende ho ita-nia família, liuliu inan-aman ka avó sira ne'ebé bele seidauk hatene di'ak kona-ba ameasa dijitál.
Talk openly about online experiences — removing shame around mistakes makes it safer to ask for help.
Ko'alia livre kona-ba esperiénsia online — halakon moe kona-ba sala sira, hodi nune'e fasil liu atu husu ajuda.
Schools and workplaces that build cyber awareness protect everyone in their network, not just individuals.
Eskola no fatin servisu ne'ebé harii konsiensia siberseguransa proteje ema hotu iha sira-nia rede, la’ós de’it ema ida-idak.
Report scams and suspicious content — your action protects the next person who might receive the same message.
Relata lalais fraude no konteúdu suspetu — ita-nia asaun ne'e proteje ema seluk ne'ebé bele simu mensajen hanesan.
A chain is only as strong as its weakest link. In a connected society, everyone's security depends on everyone else's awareness.
Korente ida nia forsa depende ba nia elo ne'ebé fraku liu. Iha sosiedade ne'ebé konektadu, seguransa ema hotu nian depende ba koñesimentu husi ema hotu.
English and Tetun do not match exactly in meaning.
Adults cyber translations: Practical guidance for parents, carers, and teachers to reduce online risk, build safer habits, and help children early when something goes wrong.
Orientasaun prátiku ba inan-aman, kuidadór no mestre sira atu hamenus risku online, harii hábitu seguru, no ajuda labarik sira sedu bainhira buat ida la'o sala.

Children are growing up online. They learn, play, chat, watch, share, and explore in digital spaces every day. That gives them opportunity, but it also exposes them to risks they are not always mature enough to recognise or manage on their own.

Labarik sira moris online lorloron. Sira aprende, halimar, ko'alia, haree, fahe, no esplora iha espasu dijitál sira. Ne'e fó oportunidade, maibé mós fó risku ne'ebé sira la sempre maduru to'o atu identifika ka jere rasik.
Tanba sa mak adultu informadu importante

Children do not just need devices and rules. They need informed adults around them. Parents and teachers are often the first people who notice when something is wrong, explain what safe behaviour looks like, and step in before a problem becomes serious.

Labarik sira la presiza de'it dispozitivu no regra. Sira presiza adultu informadu iha sira-nia leet. Inan-aman no manorin sira dala barak mak ema dahuluk ne'ebé nota bainhira buat ida la loos, esplika saida mak comportamentu seguru, no intervém antes problema ida sai grave.
Labarik sira presiza adultu ne’ebé sira fiar
If a child feels embarrassed, pressured, or frightened online, they are more likely to speak up when adults are calm, informed, and approachable.
Se labarik ida sente moe, hetan presaun, ka ta’uk online, nia sei nakloke liu ba adultu ne’ebé hakmatek, informadu, no fasil atu hakbesik.
Manorin sira bele nota padróun sedu
Teachers often notice bullying, distraction, emotional changes, or harmful sharing before a family does. Informed teachers can respond earlier and more confidently.
Manorin sira dala barak nota bullying, distraisaun, mudansa emosionál, ka fahe informasaun ne’ebé ladi’ak antes família sira nota. Manorin ne’ebé informadu bele foti asaun sedu no ho konfiansa.
Adultu sira fó ezemplu ba hábitu online.
Children copy what adults do. If adults understand privacy, oversharing, passwords, and respectful behaviour, children are more likely to learn the same habits.
Labarik sira banati-tuir saida mak adultu sira halo. Se adultu sira komprende privasidade, oversharing, password, no respeitu online, labarik sira mós sei aprende hábitu sira-ne’e.
Tanba sa mak ida-ne'e importante agora
A child can be physically safe at home and still be exposed to harm online. Adults need enough understanding to guide, notice, and respond.
Labarik ida bele seguru fízikamente iha uma, maibé sei vulnerável online. Adultu sira presiza komprende natoon atu orienta, nota, no foti asaun.
Children may not recognise grooming, scams, or manipulation until they are already under pressure.
Labarik sira bele la identifika (grooming), fraude/lasu (scams), ka manipulasaun to’o bainhira sira hetan ona presaun.
They often do not understand how much information a photo, video, or post can reveal.
Sira dala barak la komprende informasaun hira mak foto, vídeo, ka post ida bele hatudu.
They may hide a problem because they fear punishment, blame, or losing access to devices.
Sira bele subar problema tanba ta’uk hetan kastigu, kulpa, ka lakon asesu ba dispozitivu.
Online harms can affect confidence, learning, sleep, friendships, reputation, and safety in the real world.
Prejuízu online bele afeta konfiansa, aprendizajen, toba, amizade, reputasaun, no seguransa iha moris loloos.
This companion guide gives you practical activities, conversation starters, privacy checklists, and red-flag scenarios to practise with different age groups.
Guia ida-ne'e fó atividade prátiku, hahu konversa, lista kontrolu ba privasidade (privacy checklist), no senáriu perigozu atu prátika ho grupu idade diferente.
What you'll learn on this page
Saida mak ita sei aprende iha pájina ida-ne'e
Strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, updates, and screen locks.
Uza password ne'ebé forte, ativa autentikasaun ho modelu (MFA), halo atualizasaun (update) sistema beibeik, no xave ekran (lock screen) bainhira la uza.
Simple agreements, expectations, and routines that reduce risk without making technology a constant battle.
Akordu simples, espetativa, no rotina sira ne'ebé hamenus risku la halo teknolojia sai hanesan funu.
Privasidade, fahe informasaun, no sinais alerta
Safer photos, location limits, personal details, and signs that a child may need help.
Fahe foto seguru, limite lokasaun, detallu pesoál, no sinais katak labarik presiza ajuda.
Lista verifikasaun ba adultu ho etapa 8
Use unique passphrases for every account. Turn on MFA for email, banking, socials and app stores.
Uza fraze-xave úniku ba konta ida-idak. Ativa MFA ba email, banku, rede sosiál no loja aplikasaun.
Keep devices up to date. Enable automatic updates for phones, tablets, laptops and browsers.
Mantein dispozitivu sira atualizadu. Ativa atualizasaun automátika ba telefone, tablet, laptop no browser sira.
Set a device passcode or biometric lock and auto-lock under 1 minute. Turn on Find My Device.
Kria kódigu-pasajen ka biométriku no trava ekran automátiku menus husi minutu 1. Ativa 'Find My Device.'
Create a simple Family Tech Agreement or classroom digital rule set.
Kria Akordu Teknolojia Família nian ka regra dijitál ba sala-aula.
Set age-appropriate parental controls on devices, app stores and key apps. Review regularly.
Hatur kontrolu inan-aman ne'ebé tuir idade iha dispozitivu, loja aplikasaun, no aplikasaun importante sira. Reviza beibeik.
Tighten privacy on social apps. Limit who can message, tag, or see stories, and turn off precise location.
Hametin privasidade iha aplikasaun sosiál. Limita sé mak bele manda mensajen, marka (tag), ka haree story, no hamate lokasaun loloos (precise location).
Talk early and often about scams, grooming, bullying and pressure to share images. Keep conversations open.
Ko'alia sedu no beibeik kona-ba scams, grooming, bullying, no presaun atu fahe imajen. Mantén konversa nakloke.
Back up important photos and schoolwork. Know how to restore a device if needed.
Halo backup ba foto importante sira no serbisu eskola nian. Hatene oinsá atu restaura dispozitivu se presiza.
Extra child safety rules for the online world
Regra adisionál ba seguransa labarik iha mundu online
Some of the biggest risks come from information shared casually. These details may seem small, but together they can reveal far more than intended.
Risku boot balun mosu hosi informasaun ne'ebé fahe de'it. Detallu ki'ik sira-ne'e bele hatudu buat barak liu fali saida mak ita hanoin.
Do not post where a child is right now, where they go to school, what sports they play, or the places they visit regularly. Routine patterns can be used by strangers.
Keta posta labarik nia lokasaun agora, eskola ne'ebé nia ba, desportu ne'ebé nia halo, ka fatin ne'ebé nia ba beibeik. Padróun rotina bele uza hosi ema estranjeiru.
Photos can show school uniforms, street names, house numbers, car plates, landmarks, or signs in the background. Always check the full image.
Foto bele hatudu uniforme eskola, naran rua/bairo, númeru uma, númeru kareta, fatin kuñesidu ruma (landmark), ka sinal iha kotuk. Tenke cek imajen sira uluk.
Do not post drop-off times, after-school activities, tuition days, travel plans, or when children are home alone.
Keta posta tempu lori ba eskola, atividade depois escola, aula extra, planu viajen, ka bainhira labarik sira hela mesak iha uma.
Teach children not to share full name, age, school, phone number, address, passwords, or private family information in games, chats, or comments.
Hanorin labarik sira atu labele fahe naran kompletu, idade, eskola, telefone, enderesu, password, ka informasaun privadu família nian iha jogu, chat ka komentáriu.
Family or classroom digital agreement — starter points
Akordu dijitál família ka sala-aula nian — pontu inísiu
Devices are used in shared spaces where possible, especially for younger children.
Dispozitivu sira uza iha fatin ne'ebé nakloke se bele, liuliu ba labarik ki'ik sira.
Ask-before-you-click: links, downloads, and ‘free’ offers need an adult or teacher check.
Husu-molok-klik: link, download, no oferta gratuitu sira presiza adultu ka manorin sira mak haree uluk.
Pause–Think–Tell: if a message feels urgent, secret, or threatening, show an adult.
Pauza–Hanoin–Hatete: se mensajen ida sente urjente, segredu, ka ameasadu, hatudu kedas ba adultu ida.
No sharing personal details such as full name, school, address, phone, or live location.
Labele fahe detallu pesoál hanesan naran kompletu, eskola, enderesu, telefone, ka lokasaun atual.
Parental controls and account settings — quick guide
Kontrolu inan-aman no setting konta — matadalan lalais
Controls reduce risk but are not perfect. They work best when combined with conversation, trust, and regular review.
Kontrolu sira hamenus risku maibé la perfeitu. Sira di'ak liu bainhira hamutuk ho konversa, fiar, no revizaun beibeik.
Device settings: content filters, app limits, screen time, and purchase restrictions.
Setting dispozitivu: filtru konteúdu, limite app, tempu ekran, no restrisaun sosa buat ruma.
App store: require approval for installs and in-app purchases.
Loja aplikasaun: presiza aprovasaun ba instalasaun no kompras iha aplikasaun.
Home Wi‑Fi/router: family filtering and time schedules where available.
Wi-Fi uma nian: filtiraun ba família no oráriu bainhira disponivel.
Scams, grooming, and ‘too good to be true’ messages
Lia-bosok (scams), finje sai belun hodi lohi (grooming), no promesa sira ne'ebé la lojika.
Be wary of sudden money requests, codes, prizes, giveaways, or job offers. Verify through another channel.
Keta fiar lalais pedidu osan, kódigu, prémiu, ka oferta servisu ne'ebé mai derepente. Verifika fali informasaun ne'e liuhosi dalan seluk.
Teach children never to send private images. If pressured, threatened, or blackmailed, stop responding and tell an adult immediately.
Hanorin labarik sira atu keta haruka foto privadu ba ema seluk. Se ema ruma ameasa ka obriga sira, husu sira atu keta responde ona no konta kedas ba inan-aman ka ema boot ne'ebé sira fiar.
Keep evidence such as screenshots and URLs. Report in-app and block the account.
Rai evidénsia hanesan screenshot no link (URL). Report ka fó hatene iha aplikasaun laran, no blokeia kedas konta ne'e.
Sinais alerta ne'ebé inan-aman ka ema boot sira tenke atensaun
A child suddenly hides screens, changes mood after going online, or becomes secretive about a contact.
Labarik derepente subar telefone ka laptop, sira-nia laran-triste ka hirus fali depois uza internet, no sai segredu liu kona-ba ema foun ne'ebé sira ko'alia ba.
A new 'friend' quickly moves the chat to private messages or asks for secrecy.
Kolega 'foun' ida ne'ebé dudu lalais atu ko'alia de'it iha 'inbox' (mensajen privadu) no husu atu keta fó hatene ema seluk.
Messages become pushy, sexual, manipulative, or ask for images, money, or meetings.
Mensajen sira ne'ebé dudu ho kloot, ko'alia buat fo'er (sexual), obriga, lohi, ka husu foto, osan, no obriga atu hasoru malu
A child seems frightened about getting in trouble and avoids telling adults what happened.
"Labarik sai ta'uk tanba sente katak nia halo sala ka sei hetan si'ak, nune'e nia subar fali saida mak akontese loloos."
Prefer mobile data hotspot for logins. Avoid banking on café Wi‑Fi.
"Uza liu hotspot (dadus telemóvel) hodi login. Keta loke aplikasaun banku nian uza Wi-Fi iha kafé ka fatin públiku."
Log out after use and turn off auto-connect on children’s devices.
"Sempre log out depois uza, no hamate 'auto-connect' iha labarik sira-nia handphone ka tablet."
Support first. Stay calm and thank the child for telling you.
"Suporta sira uluk. Hakmatek no fó agradese ba labarik tanba brani atu konta problema ne'e ba ita."
Collect evidence safely: screenshots, URLs, usernames, timestamps.
"Rai evidénsia ho seguru: foti screenshot, rai link (URL), naran user, no mós oras akontesimentu."
Change passwords, enable MFA, and sign out other sessions.
"Troka password kedas, ativa autentikasaun (MFA), no log out hosi kualkér fatin seluk ne'ebé ita-nia konta nakloke hela."
Block and report in the app or platform.
Blokeia no relata iha aplikasaun ka plataforma.
Contact your telco, school, bank, or local authority if money, threats, or identity details are involved.
"Kontaktu operadór telkom, eskola, banku, ka Polísia se kazu ne'e envolve osan, ameasa fíziku, ka na'ok identidade."
These official guides show step-by-step settings and useful talking points for families and educators.
"Matadalan ofisiál sira-ne'e hatudu pasu-ba-pasu oinsá atu halo setting no fó mós ideia oinsá inan-aman ho profesór sira bele ko'alia ho labarik sira."
Protect your children online — Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC)
"Proteje ita-nia oan iha internet — Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC)"
Secure your user account (including parental controls) — ACSC
"Proteje ita-nia konta uza-na'in (inklui kontrolu ba inan-aman) — ACSC."
eSafety Guide — common apps, games and sites
"Matadalan eSafety — aplikasaun, jogu, no sítiu sira ne'ebé baibain uza."
"Download poster ka lista kontrolu ba uma nian (sei fali mai iha tempu badak)."
This page provides original Lafaek guidance and links to ACSC/eSafety for optional deeper learning.
"Pájina ida-ne'e fó matadalan orijinál husi Lafaek no ligasaun (link) ba ACSC/eSafety hodi aprende liután."
Adults interactive guide: "Matadalan Interativu ba Inan-Aman no Kuidadór sira."
Short, practical activities to help you talk with kids about passwords, boundaries, privacy settings, and strangers online.
"Atividade badak no prátiku hodi ajuda ita ko'alia ho labarik sira kona-ba password, limite (boundary), setting privasidade, no ema foun iha internet."
Pick an age band, then choose a topic. These scripts keep the tone calm, firm, and not weird. (We’re aiming for “helpful adult”, not “internet detective”.)
"Hili idade labarik nian, depois hili tópiku ida. Lia-fuan sira-ne'e ajuda ita ko'alia ho kalma, firme, no klaru. (Ita-nia objetivu mak atu sai 'ema boot ne'ebé ajuda', la'ós 'detetive internet'.)"
"Privasidade no keta fahe buat barak liu (oversharing)".
"Ema foun, foto privadu, no hasoru malu".
This isn’t about being perfect. It’s about raising the effort level for attackers. A long passphrase beats a short “clever” password.
"Ita la presiza sai perfeitu. Objetivu mak atu halo ema aat sira susar liu atu lohi ita. Fraze segredu (passphrase) ne'ebé naruk, seguru liu fali password badak ne'ebé ita hanoin 'matenek' uitoan."
"Saida mak ita bele aprende husi ezemplu ne'e"
Longer phrases are harder to guess and easier to remember. The goal: unique passphrase per account + MFA on the important ones (email, social, banking).

"Fraze naruk (passphrase) fasil atu lembra maibé susar ba ema seluk atu si'ik. Objetivu mak: uza fraze segredu ne'ebé la hanesan ba konta ida-idak + ativa MFA ba konta sira ne'ebé importante (email, média sosiál, no banku)."
Select a few rules that suit your home. Keep it realistic: rules you won’t enforce become jokes.
"Hili regra balu ne'ebé loos ba ita-nia uma. Kria regra ne'ebé ita bele duni halo: regra ne'ebé ita rasik la tuir ka la kumpri, ikusmai sei sai de'it hanesan ko’alia-halimar"
"Akordu simples ba família kona-ba uza teknolojia."
These are the settings that usually cause trouble. Toggle them on/off to see the risk note.
"Setting sira-ne'e mak baibain kria problema. Hili 'on' ka 'off' hodi haree nota kona-ba ninia risku."
These are common messages kids get. Tap an answer to see the best move.
"Ne'e mak mensajen sira ne'ebé labarik sira baibain simu. Hili resposta ida hodi haree asaun saida mak di'ak liu."
If a child is pressured for images or threatened (sextortion): stop responding, save evidence, report/block, and get support immediately.
"Se labarik hetan presaun hodi haruka foto privadu ka simu ameasa (sextortion): para ona hatán chat, rai evidénsia hotu, report no blokeia kedas, no buka tulun hosi ema boot ka autoridade."
If something feels wrong: show a trusted adult immediately.
Se buat ruma sente laloos: hatudu kedas ba adultu ida ne'ebé ita fiar.
This setting reduces risk and makes problems less likely.
"Setting ida-ne'e hamenus risku no halo problema la mosu fasil."
"⚠️ Ne'e la'ós dalan ne'ebé di'ak liu"
"Kalan labele uza telemóvel ka laptop iha kuartu laran."
Ask an adult before clicking links or installing apps
"Husu ema boot antes klik link ruma ka instala aplikasaun foun."
Pause–Think–Tell if a message feels urgent/secret
"Para uitoan–Hanoin–Konta ba ema boot se mensajen ida sente urjente ka segredu."
"Keta fahe naran kompletu, naran eskola, hela-fatin, ka númeru telefone."
No meetups with online people without an adult
"Labele hasoru malu ho ema ne'ebé koñese de'it iha internet se la lori ema boot."
"Keta haruka foto privadu ba ema seluk."
Only approved friends can see posts.
"Kolega ne'ebé ita simu ona mak bele haree buat ne'ebé ita post."

"Se konta ne'e públiku, ema foun bele foti informasaun kona-ba ita ho fasil."
Allow DMs only from friends/approved people.
"Permite de'it kolega ne'ebé ita koñese atu haruka mensajen."
"Se lori DM 'loke hela', ema laran aat sira bele uza hodi lohi ita (scam) ka tenta besik labarik sira (grooming)."
Don’t show it publicly in profiles.
"Keta hatudu informasaun sira-ne'e iha ita-nia profile públiku."
"Hatudu kontatu iha fatin públiku sei konvida mensajen arbiru (spam) no Lohi/Fraude (scam) barak."
"Hamate ita-nia pozisaun lolos iha fatin online (Precise Location)."
Turn off precise location in social/gaming apps.

"Loke hela 'pozisaun lolos' iha online bele halo ema foun monitoriza ita-nia movementu."
"Ita tenke fó aprovasaun uluk mak ema bele tag ita iha sira-nia foto."

Don’t auto-upload address book to apps.

Try this: “We use secret words (passphrases) to lock our accounts. We never share them, even with friends. If you forget, you tell me — you don’t guess.”

Follow-up: “Let’s make one together: 3 random words + a number.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ita uza fraze segredu (passphrase) hodi xave ita-nia konta sira. Ita nunka fahe ba ema seluk, maske ba kolega sira. Se ó haluha, fó hatene ha'u — keta si'ik arbiru.”

Tuir mai: “Mai ita kria hamutuk ida: lia-fuan foun 3 ne'ebé la hanesan + númeru ida.”

Try this: “Your email is the master key. If someone gets it, they can reset everything. Let’s set a unique passphrase and turn on 2-step verification.”

Keep it simple: “You remember it, I don’t need it. We can store recovery codes safely.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ita-nia email ne'e hanesan xave boot. Se ema ruma foti, sira bele loke buat hotu. Mai ita kria fraze segredu ne'ebé úniku no ativa verifikasaun pasu-2 (2-step verification).”

Halo simples de'it: “Ó mak tenke lembra, ha'u la presiza hatene. Ita bele rai de'it 'kódigu rekupersaun' iha fatin ne'ebé seguru.”
Try this: “I’m not here to police you. I’m here to make your accounts harder to hijack. Unique passphrases + MFA on email/social/banking. If you want, we can set it up together in 10 minutes.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ha'u la'ós atu sai polísia ba ó. Ha'u iha ne'e atu halo ó-nia konta sira susar ba ema atu na'ok. Fraze segredu ne'ebé úniku + MFA iha email/média sosiál/banku. Se ó hakarak, ita bele halo hamutuk durante minutu 10 de'it.”
Try this: “When you post, you’re telling the world where you are and who you’re with. We keep accounts private and we don’t share our school, address, or phone number.”
"Koko ida-ne'e: “Bainhira ó post buat ruma, ó fó hatene mundu katak ó iha ne'ebé no ó ho sé. Ita tenke uza konta privadu no keta fahe naran eskola, hela-fatin, ka númeru telefone.”"
Try this: “Privacy settings are like curtains. Open curtains = everyone can look in. Let’s check: who can message you, tag you, and see your stories. Also: turn off precise location.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Definisaun privasidade ne'e hanesan kortina. Kortina nakloke = ema hotu bele hateke ba laran. Mai ita kontrolla: sé mak bele haruka mensajen, tag ó, no haree ó-nia story. No mós: hamate 'fatin lolos' (precise location).”
Try this: “Oversharing isn’t just photos. It’s patterns: where you go, when you’re alone, what you buy. Let’s tighten settings and keep location off unless you truly need it.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Bainhira ita fahe buat barak liu ne'e la'ós de'it kona-ba foto. Ne'e mós kona-ba ita-nia atividade: ó ba ne'ebé, bainhira mak ó mesak, no saida mak ó sosa. Mai ita kontrola setting sira no hamate 'fatin lolos' (location) se ó la presiza duni.”
Try this: “We have family rules: devices charge in the living room at night, and if something feels scary or secret, you show me right away.”
"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ita iha regra família nian: kalan dispozitivu hotu tenke charge iha sala-de-vizita, no se buat ruma sente ta'uk ka segredu, ó tenke hatudu kedas ba ha'u.”
Try this: “I’m not banning your phone. I’m setting safety rules. If someone asks for secrets, images, or to meet up, you pause and tell me. No trouble for telling the truth.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ha'u la'ós bandu ó uza telemóvel. Ha'u kria regra seguransa de'it. Se ema ruma husu segredu, husu foto, ka husu atu hasoru malu, ó para uitoan no fó hatene ha'u. Ó sei la simu problema tanba ko'alia lia-loos.”
Try this: “You’re getting older, so rules become agreements. The one non-negotiable: if you’re pressured, threatened, or asked to meet, you loop me in.”
"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ó boot ona, tanba ne'e regra sira duni sai tiha akordu hamutuk. Buat ida ne'ebé labele nega mak: se ema ruma fó presaun, ameasa, ka husu atu hasoru malu, ó tenke fó hatene ha'u.”
Try this: “People online can pretend to be kids. We don’t send photos to strangers, and we never meet anyone without an adult.”
"Koko ida-ne'e: “Ema iha internet bele finje sai fali labarik. Ita labele haruka foto ba ema ne'ebé ita la koñese, no ita nunka hasoru ema ruma se la lori ema boot.”
Try this: “If anyone asks for images, tries to move you to WhatsApp/Telegram fast, or says ‘don’t tell’, that’s a red flag. Screenshot, block, report, tell me.”

"Koko ida-ne'e: “Se ema ruma husu foto, tenta dudu ó atu ko'alia fali iha WhatsApp/Telegram ho lalais, ka dehan 'keta fó hatene ema', ne'e sinal perigu. Screenshot, blokeia, report, no fó hatene ha'u.”
Try this: “If someone tries to sexualise chat, asks for images, or threatens you: stop replying. Save evidence. Report. We’ll handle it together. You won’t be punished for coming to me.”
"Koko ida-ne'e: “Se labarik ida hetan ameasa ka kestionamentu seksuál: para ona responde. Rai evidénsia hodi relata. Ita sei rezolve hamutuk. Ó la sala tanba mai fó-hatene ha'u."
A DM says: “Don’t tell anyone. It’s just us. Send a photo to prove you trust me.”
"Ema ida iha inbox direita dehan: “Labele hatete ba ema seluk. Ita de'it. Haruka foto ida atu prova katak ó fiar ha’u.”
Send the photo so you don’t lose the friendship.
"Haruka foto atu nune'e ó sei la lakon kolega."
Explain you’re not comfortable, then keep chatting quietly.
"Esplika katak ó sente la konfortável, depois kontinua ko'alia de'it subasubar."
Stop. Screenshot. Block. Report. Tell a trusted adult.
"Para. Screenshot. Blokeia. Relata. Fó-hatene ba adultu ne’ebé ita fiar."
Start a new chat account to avoid trouble.
"Kria konta chat foun hodi hasees aan hosi problema."
Secrets + pressure for images is a major red flag. The safest move: stop responding, keep evidence, block/report, and get support immediately.
"Konversa segredu + presaun atu haruka foto mak sinal perigu boot (red flag). Dalan ne'ebé seguru liu mak: para ona hatán, rai evidénsia (screenshot), blokeia/reporta, no buka tulun kedas."
Someone online says: “Let’s meet at a café. Come alone. No need to tell your parents.”
"Ema ida online dehan: “Ita hasoru malu iha kafé. Mai mesak de'it. La presiza hatete ba inan-aman.”
Say yes because a café is public.
“Dehan ‘sin’ tanba hanoin katak kafé ne’e fatin públiku ida.”
Ask for their ‘real name’, then go alone.
“Husu nia naran loos, depois bá hasoru nia mesak de’it.”
Say no. Any meet-up requires an adult and a safety plan.
"Hatete: la bele. Hasoru malu fízikamente presiza adultu ida no planu seguru."
"Fahe lokasaun atuál atu nune'e fasil atu buka malu."
Any request to meet alone + keep it secret is a red flag. If a meet-up ever happens, it must involve a trusted adult, a public place, and clear safety steps.
"Husu atu hasoru mesak + subar hosi ema seluk = risku no perigozu. Se atu hasoru malu duni, tenke lori adultu ida, iha fatin públiku, no iha asaun seguru ne'ebé klaru."
A message says: “Support team here. Send the verification code (OTP) you just received.”
"Mensajen dehan: “Ami ekipa suportu. Haruka kódigu verifikasaun (OTP) ne'ebé ó simu agora.”
Send the code because it says ‘support’.
Never share OTPs. Verify through the official app/site.
"Keta fahe OTP. Verifika de'it liu hosi aplikasaun ka sítiu (website) ofisiál."
Send your password to prove ownership.
"Haruka password hodi prova katak konta ne'e ó-nian."
Click the link to fix it fast.
Klik link ne'ebé sira haruka hodi rezolve lalais problema ne'e."
One-time codes are basically keys. Real support will not ask for them. Use official channels to check account alerts and security settings.
"OTP ne’e hanesan xave. Ekipa suportu loloos sei la husu kódigu ne’e. Verifika de'it liu husi kanál ofisiál."

Youth cyber security: Hardcoded in page heading; same in both versions
Stay Sharp. Stay Safe. Stay Online.
Your phone and laptop are your life — class, work, payments, friends. These moves strengthen your security and privacy without slowing you down.
Ita-nia telemovel no laptop mak parte boot iha ita-nia moris — klase, servisu, selu no ko'alia ho kolega. Passu sira-ne'e hadia seguransa no privasidade la'o lalais.
Practice these skills in mission mode. Prove your rank.
Prátika kbiit sira iha misaun. Hatudu ita-nia rank.
Phishing, impersonation, fake job offers, sextortion, giveaway traps.
Phishing, ema halo hanesan kolega, oferta la loos, sextortion no isku prémiu.
Strong passwords, passkeys/2FA, recovery codes, device locks.
Password forte, passkeys/2FA, kódigu rekuperasaun, tranka dispozitivu.
Tighten social settings, limit location data, reduce oversharing.
Hadrek setting sosiál, limita lokasaun no labele fahe buat barak liu.
Use a different password for every account. Turn on 2FA and use an app, passkey, or biometrics if possible.
Uza password diferente ba kada konta. Liga 2FA no uza app, passkey, ka biometria se bele.
Add backup codes and a second email. If you get locked out, recovery matters more than panic.
Tau backup code no email segundu. Se ita lakon asesu, rekuperasaun mak importante.
Limit who sees posts, stories, your friend list, and your phone number.
Limita sé maka bele haree post, story, lista kolega no numeru telefone.
Do not share live location, school schedules, ID details, or travel plans.
Keta fahe lokasaun atual, oráriu eskola, detallu ID ka planu viajen.
Be suspicious of money requests, too-good-to-be-true offers, or emotional pressure.
Deskonfia mensajen ne'ebé husu osan, oferta di'ak liu, ka presaun emosionál.
Check sender address, inspect links, and contact the person another way if needed.
Haree remetente, verifika link, no kontaktu ema ida-ne'e liu husi dalan seluk se presiza.
Use a hotspot if possible. Avoid logging into sensitive accounts on public networks.
Uza hotspot se bele. Evita login ba konta importante iha rede públiku.
Update your phone, apps, and browser. Delete apps you never use.
Atualiza telemovel, app no browser. Hamoos app sira ne'ebé ita la uza.
Check videos, images, and voice messages for odd details, fake urgency, and accounts that look new or suspicious.
Haree vídeo, imajen, no voz sira ba detallu estranho, urjénsia falsu, no konta foun ka suspeitu sira.
Tap each step to confirm you understand it.
Taka kada etapa atu konfirma katak ita kompriende.
Pause on surprise messages, giveaways, or urgent requests. Do not react straight away.
Para bainhira simu mensajen surpreza, prezente ka pedidu urgente. La presiza responde kedas.
Good. Pausing stops panic-clicking and gives you time to think.
Di'ak. Para uluk evita klik tanba pániku.
What are they asking for — money, codes, photos, passwords? Could this be impersonation?
Saida mak sira husu — osan, kódigu, foto, password? Bele impostór ka lae?
Good. Scammers want speed. Thinking slows them down.
Di'ak. Fraudador sira hakarak ita halo lalais, la'ós hanoin kle'an.
Verify through another channel. Inspect the URL. Search if others report the same scam.
Verifika liu husi kanál seluk. Haree URL. Buka se ema seluk relata fraude hanesan.
Good. A second check catches fake links, fake people, and fake urgency.
Di'ak. Chekagem ida tan ajuda deteta link falsu no ema falsu.
✓ PROTOCOL CONFIRMED — YOU'RE READY TO OPERATE
✓ PROTOKOLU KONFIRMA ONA — ITA PRONTU ATU OPERA
Unnatural blinking, odd lighting, or accessories that shift between frames.
Piska matan la natural, naroman estrañu ka objetu ne'ebé muda iha frame sira.
Lip-sync lag or robotic voice cadence that does not match the emotion.
Boca no lian la hanesan ka lian hanesan robô la tuir emosaun.
Message creates urgency, fear, or flattery — designed to make you act fast.
Mensajen kria urjénsia, tauk ka elojia atu halo ita atua lalais.
Account is new, unverified, or shares zero mutual connections.
Konta foun, seidauk verifika ka la iha kolega komun.
Use a password manager — or a personal system. Every account needs a unique password.
Uza password manager ka sistema pesoál. Kada konta tenke iha password úniku.
Enable biometrics or passcode on all devices. Set auto-lock under 1 minute.
Liga biometria ka passcode iha dispozitivu hotu. Auto-lock menus husi minutu ida.
Activate 2FA / passkeys for email, banking, socials, and cloud storage.
Ativa 2FA / passkeys ba email, banku, rede sosiál no cloud storage.
Register for Find My Device. Test it once to confirm it works.
Rejista Find My Device. Testa dala ida atu konfirma funsiona.
Set personal accounts to private. Audit tagged photos and old posts.

Screenshot everything. Save evidence before it disappears.

Change passwords immediately. Revoke suspicious sessions. Enable 2FA.

Report and block the account in-app.

Tell a trusted adult, mentor, or your institution's IT support.
Fó-hatene ba adultu konfiadu, mentor ka IT suporte iha ita-nia instituisaun.

AI tools can now create fake photos, fake videos, and even fake voices that look very convincing. Some people use these tools creatively and responsibly, but others use them to trick, embarrass, scam, manipulate, or spread lies.

Agora dadaun, ferramenta AI sira bele kria foto falsu, vídeo falsu, no voz falsu ne'ebé bele haree convincente tebes. Ema balu uza sira atu halo buat kreativu no responsavel, maibé ema seluk uza atu bosok, moe, scam, manipula, ka espalha lia sala.

That means we need to be more careful than before about trusting everything we see online. A photo or video is no longer proof on its own. We need to slow down, look closely, and ask where it came from, who shared it, and whether it makes sense.

Ne'e signifika katak agora ita tenke kuidadu liu tan bainhira haree foto no vídeo online. Foto ka vídeo ida de'it la'ós prova suficiente. Ita tenke para, haree didi'ak, no husu hosi ne'ebé mak mai, sé mak fahe, no se nia faz sentidu ka lae.

Eyes, blinking, or reflections that feel slightly wrong

Hair, earrings, glasses, or background details that blur or shift strangely

Blink la natural / Refleksaun iha matan
AI faces sometimes have strange reflections in the eyes or blinking patterns that feel slightly off.
Dala ruma oin ne'ebé AI halo iha refleksaun estranho iha matan ka blink ne'ebé la sente natural.
Look carefully near the edges. AI often bends or distorts backgrounds slightly.
Haree didi'ak liu iha beira sira. AI dala barak halo kotuk imajen torce ka muda badak.
Skin may look too perfect, almost like plastic. Real skin has texture and tiny imperfections.
Kulit bele haree perfeitu liu hanesan plastik. Kulit loos iha textura no detallu ki'ik sira.
Strands may blend into the background or look slightly blurry or inconsistent.
Ruma fuuk sira bele mistura ho kotuk imajen ka haree blur no la konsistente.

Mission Focus: Human mistakes are the biggest attack surface.
Foku Misaun: sala umanu mak fatin risku boot liu.
Tip: attackers usually target people before they target devices.
Sujestaun: atacante sira alvu ema uluk antes alvu dispozitivu.
Checking for account theft tactics... FOUND
Result: human pressure tactics detected. Run "scam" or "verify".
Rezultadu: taktika presaun umanu detetadu. Halo "scam" ka "verify".
1) Never share OTP or verification codes.
1) Keta fahe OTP ka kódigu verifikasaun.
2) Real prizes do not require surprise payment fees.
2) Prémiu loos la presiza taxa surpresa.
3) Unknown short links should be treated as suspicious.
3) Short link ne'ebé la hatene tenke konsidera suspetu.
4) Urgency is often used to stop you from thinking.
4) Urjénsia dala barak uza atu para ita hanoin.
5) If the message triggers panic or excitement, slow down.
5) Se mensajen kria pániku ka anima boot liu, para uluk.
If someone asks for your code, they may be logging in as you.
Se ema husu ita-nia kódigu, bele nia tama hanesan ita.
Legitimate support does not ask for OTP codes.
If you shared one: change password, enable 2FA, check active sessions.
Se ita fahe ona: troka password, ativa 2FA, no haree sesaun ativu.
Do not trust a short link just because a friend sent it.
Keta fiar short link de'it tanba kolega haruka.
Husu uluk link ne'e kona-ba saida.
Open the official site or app yourself instead of tapping the link.
Di'ak liu loke app ka site ofisiál rasik.
Do not prepay to 'hold' an item.
Meet in a safe public place.
Hasoru iha fatin públiku ne'ebé seguru.
Bring an adult or trusted person if needed.
Lori adultu ka ema konfiadu ida se presiza.
STOP: pause when something feels urgent, weird, or exciting.
PARA: para bainhira buat ida sente urgente, estranhu, ka anima boot liu.
THINK: what are they asking for — money, codes, photos, passwords?
HANOÍN: sira husu saida — osan, kódigu, foto, password?
CHECK: verify through another channel, inspect the URL, ask someone you trust.
CHEKA: verifika liu husi kanál seluk, haree URL, husu ema ne'ebé ita fiar.
Youth Cyber Privacy: Goal: reduce what strangers can learn about you and make account takeovers harder.
Objetivu: hamenus buat ne'ebé ema estranjeiru sira bele aprende kona-ba ita no halo roubu konta sai susar liu.
English/Tetun labels refer to slightly different page names
Open a setting card to learn why it matters.
Loke karta setting ida atu aprende tanba sa mak importante.
Use the switch inside each card to make it safer.
Uza switch iha karta ida-idak atu halo seguru liu.
Try to reduce your overall risk score.
Koko atu hamenus ita-nia pontu risku jerál.
Hatur auto-lock menus husi minutu ida.
Tip: if a setting is hard to find, search inside the app for “privacy”, “security”, or “permissions”.
Sujestaun: se susar atu buka setting ida, buka iha app laran ho liafuan “privacy”, “security”, ka “permissions”.
Good privacy defaults + strong account protection are active.
Privasidade di'ak no protesaun konta forte ativu ona.
There are still gaps. Reduce exposed data and strengthen logins.
Sei iha lacuna sira. Hamenus data ne'ebé sai no hametin login sira.
Apps can track where you are live or over time.
App sira bele tuir iha ne'ebé ita iha agora ka iha tempu nia laran.
Location data can expose routines such as home, school, work, and regular hangouts. That can make stalking, impersonation, or timing-based scams easier.
Dadus lokasaun bele hatudu rotina sira hanesan uma, eskola, servisu, no fatin ne'ebé ita ba beibeik. Ne'e bele fasilita stalking, falsifikasaun identidade, ka scam ne'ebé depende ba oras.
Turn off precise location for social apps.
Hamate precise location ba app sosiál sira.
Remove location access from apps that do not need it.
Hasai asesu lokasaun hosi app sira ne'ebé la presiza.
Your profile is visible to strangers.
Ita-nia perfil bele haree hosi ema estranjeiru sira.
Public profiles make it easier for people to collect your photos, friends, personal details, and habits. That information can be used for scams or fake accounts.
Perfil públiku fasilita ema atu halibur ita-nia foto, kolega sira, detallu pesoál, no hábitu sira. Informasaun ne'e bele uza ba scam ka konta falsu.
Make your account private or friends-only.
Halo ita-nia konta privadu ka ba kolega de'it.
Hide phone number and email from the profile.
Subar numeru telefone no email hosi perfil.
Hide your friends list if the platform allows it.
Subar lista kolega se plataforma permite.
Others can tag you without approval.
Ema seluk bele tag ita laiha aprovasaun.
Tags can connect your name to places, people, and events, even when you did not choose to share that information yourself.
Tag sira bele liga ita-nia naran ba fatin, ema, no eventu sira, maski ita la hili atu fahe informasaun ne'e rasik.
Presiza aprovasaun molok tag sira mosu.
Limita sé maka bele tag ita.
Review older tagged posts and remove risky ones.
Reviza post tag tuan sira no hasai sira ne'ebé risku.
Platforms and partners use your data for targeting.
Plataforma no parseiru sira uza ita-nia dadus ba target.
This is not just about ads. Data sharing builds a profile of your interests, habits, and vulnerabilities over time.
Ida-ne'e la'ós kona-ba anúnsiu de'it. Partilha dadus halo perfil ida kona-ba ita-nia interese, hábitu, no vulnerabilidade sira iha tempu.
Opt out of partner data sharing where possible.
Sai hosi partilha dadus ho parseiru sira se bele.
Extra login protection beyond your password.
Protesaun login adisionál liu husi password.
If someone gets your password, 2FA or passkeys can stop them from taking your account.
Se ema ida hetan ita-nia password, 2FA ka passkeys bele para sira atu la toma ita-nia konta.
Turn on 2FA using an authenticator app if possible.
Ativa 2FA ho authenticator app se bele.
Rai backup code iha fatin seguru.
Secure your email account first because it resets everything else.
Segura email konta uluk tanba nia reset buat hotu seluk.
Passcode, auto-lock, and biometrics on your phone.
Passcode, auto-lock, no biometria iha ita-nia telefone.
If your device is unlocked, many other protections become useless because the person already has direct access.
Se ita-nia dispozitivu loke hela, protesaun barak seluk sai menus útil tanba ema ne'e iha asesu direta ona.
Set a strong passcode, not 0000 or a birthday.
Hatur passcode forte, la'ós 0000 ka data moris.
Ativa auto-lock menus husi minutu ida.
Enable Find My Device and test it once.
Ativa Find My Device no testa dala ida.
Youth Cyber Social: Train your brain to spot pressure tactics, impersonation, and “act now” traps.
Treina ita-nia hanoin atu deteta presaun, falsifikasaun identidade, no armadilha “halo agora”.
How confident are you right now?
Tip: scammers want panic. Slow down and verify.
Sujestaun: fraudador sira hakarak pániku. Para uluk no verifika.
Rule: if it asks for money, codes, photos, or secrets — verify using another channel.
Regra: se husu osan, kódigu, foto, ka segredu — verifika liu hosi kanál seluk.
Social engineering means tricking people into giving away information, money, or access. Defence habit: slow down → verify → refuse secrets, codes, money, and pressure.
Social engineering mak bainhira ema bosok ita atu fó informasaun, osan, ka asesu. Hábitu defesa mak: para → verifika → rejeita segredu, kódigu, osan, no presaun.
“I don’t do secrets or codes online.”
“Hau la halo segredu ka fó kódigu online.”
Then: screenshot → block → report → tell someone you trust.
Depois: screenshot → blokeia → relata → hatete ba ema ida ne'ebé ita fiar.
Para. Verifika liu hosi dalan seluk.
If it asks for money or codes…
Hanoin katak scam to'o prova katak lae.
Change password, enable 2FA, report it, and get help.
Troka password, ativa 2FA, relata, no buka ajuda.
You were confident and correct. Good spotting.
Ita konfiansa no loos. Di'ak tebes ita deteta.
You were cautious and still got it right. That’s a good habit.
Ita kuidadu no loos nafatin. Ida-ne'e hábitu di'ak.
You were confident but the answer was risky. Slow down next time.
Ita konfiansa maibé resposta ne'e risku. Tuir mai para uluk no hanoin.
You were unsure, which is better than rushing. Now check the clues.
Ita seidauk certeza, ne'e di'ak liu duké halo lalais. Agora haree pista sira.

Consider translating label too; currently hardcoded in English.
Consider translating label too; currently hardcoded in English.
You receive a DM from a friend: "Hey! I’m stuck at the airport and lost my wallet. Can you send me $50 via GiftCard? I’ll pay you back tomorrow!"
Ita simu DM ida hosi kolega: "Hey! Hau hela iha airport no lakon carteira. Bele haruka $50 liu husi GiftCard? Aban hau sei selu fila fali!"
It asks for gift cards, which are hard to trace and hard to recover.
Husu gift card, ne'ebé susar atu tuir no susar atu fila.
It pushes you to act fast, with no time to verify.
Presaun atu halo lalais, laiha tempu atu verifika.
It uses emotion like panic or guilt to pressure you.
Uza emosaun hanesan pániku ka kulpa atu pressiona ita.
Verify another way, like calling, voice note, or asking in person.
Verifika liu husi dalan seluk hanesan telefonema, voice note, ka hasoru malu.
Ask a question only your real friend would know.
Husu pergunta ida ne'ebé kolega loos de'it bele hatene.
If it is real, use a safer method after verification.
Se loos, uza dalan seguru liu depois de verifika.
Send the money immediately to help.
This is a classic hacked-account scam. Trust is what makes people move too fast.
Ne'e scam klasiku ida hosi konta ne'ebé hacked. Fiar mak halo ema sira atua lalais demais.
Call your friend or ask for a voice note to verify.
Telefonema ba kolega ka husu voice note atu verifika.
Correct. Checking through a different channel breaks the scammer’s advantage.
Loos. Verifika liu husi kanál seluk estraga avantajen fraudador nian.
Ask for their bank details instead.
Still risky. If the account is compromised, the scammer is still replying to you.
Sei risku nafatin. Se konta ne'e komprometidu, fraudador mak sei responde ba ita.
You get an email from "Insta-Support": "Copyright Violation Detected. Click here to appeal or your account will be deleted in 24 hours."
Ita simu email hosi "Insta-Support": "Copyright Violation Detected. Klik iha ne'e atu apela ka ita-nia konta sei apaga iha oras 24."
Ameaça katak ita sei lakon buat ida iha oras 24.
It pushes you toward a link.
Koko lori ita ba link ida.
The sender address often does not match the real company.
Remetente dala barak la hanesan ho kompanhia loos.
Do not click the email link.
Keta klik link iha email ne'e.
Open the real app or site yourself and check notifications.
Loke app ka site loos rasik no haree notifikasaun sira.
Check the sender address and report phishing.
Haree remetente didi'ak no relata hanesan phishing.
Panic and click the link to appeal.
Pániku no klik link atu apela.
That is exactly what the scam wants. Panic makes people skip checking the URL.
Ne'e loos duni saida mak scam ne'e hakarak. Pániku halo ema la verifika URL.
Reply to the email asking for proof.
Hatán email ne'e no husu prova.
Replying confirms your email is active and keeps you engaged with the scam.
Hatán email konfirma katak ita-nia email aktivu no halo ita sei envolve ho scam ne'e.
Check the sender address and log in through the real app or site yourself.
Haree remetente no tama liu husi app ka site loos rasik.
Correct. Always go to the real app or website yourself instead of trusting the email link.
Loos. Sempre ba app ka website loos rasik iha fatin fiar de'it iha link hosi email.
A popular influencer posts: "I’m giving away 1000 ETH! Send 0.1 ETH to verify your wallet and get 10x back!"
Influencer populár ida posta: "Hau sei fó 1000 ETH! Haruka 0.1 ETH atu verifika ita-nia wallet no simu fila 10x!"
It asks you to pay first to ‘verify’.
Comments and social proof can be fake or bots.
Komentáriu no prova sosiál bele falsu ka bot sira.
Never pay to receive a prize.
Keta selu atu simu prémiu ida.
If money is involved, assume scam until proven otherwise.
Se envolve osan, hanoin katak scam to'o prova katak lae.
This is a classic doubling scam. Real giveaways do not ask you to pay first.
Ne'e scam klasiku ida atu duplica osan. Giveaway loos la husu selu uluk.
Report the post as a scam.
Correct. Reporting helps protect other people too.
Loos. Relata ajuda proteje ema seluk mós.
Wait to see if others get paid.
Hein haree se ema seluk simu loos ka lae.
Scammers can fake comments and fake proof. Waiting does not make it safer.
Fraudador sira bele halo komentáriu no prova falsu. Hein de'it la halo ne'e seguru liu.
A message says: "I’m from support. To secure your account, send me the code you just received."
Mensajen ida dehan: "Hau hosi support. Atu seguru ita-nia konta, haruka mai kódigu ne'ebé ita simu foin."
Real support never asks for one-time codes.
Support loos la husu kódigu ida-ne'e.
That code can reset your password or confirm a login.
Kódigu ne'e bele reset password ka confirma login.
They are trying to access your account right now.
Sira daudaun ne'e koko asesu ita-nia konta.
Never share OTP or verification codes.
Keta fahe OTP ka kódigu verifikasaun.
Change password and enable 2FA if needed.
Troka password no ativa 2FA se presiza.
Send the code so they can ‘secure’ the account.
Haruka kódigu atu sira bele ‘segura’ konta.
That code is what lets them steal the account. It is not protection.
Kódigu ne'e mak dalan sira atu na'ok konta. Ida-ne'e la'ós protesaun.
Ignore the message and report it. Keep your codes private.
Ignora mensajen ne'e no relata. Rai ita-nia kódigu privadu.
Correct. Verification codes are for you only. If someone asks, treat it as a scam.
Loos. Kódigu verifikasaun ba ita de'it. Se ema husu, trata hanesan scam.
Ask them to prove who they are first.
Husu sira prova sé mak sira uluk.
You do not need to negotiate. Real support would not ask for the code at all.
La presiza ko'alia naruk. Support loos la husu kódigu ida-ne'e atu hahú kedas.

Go through your CV with this checklist before every application.
Verifika CV ho lista ida-ne'e molok aplikasaun hotu-hotu.
Update one section of your CV
Prátika resposta entrevista 3 iha lian aas
Your CV is your first impression. It should be clear, tailored to the job, and easy for an employer to scan in 30 seconds.
Ita-nia CV mak impresaun primeiru. Tenke klaru, tuir vaga ida, no empregadór bele lee iha segundu 30.
Tailor your CV to every job — never send the same version twice.
Hadia CV ba kargu ida-idak — keta haruka versaun hanesan ba fatin hotu.
Put your strongest, most relevant experience at the top.
Tau esperiénsia ne'ebé forte no relevante iha leten.
Use action words: created, managed, designed, organised, supported, delivered.
Uza liafuan asaun: kria, jere, dezeña, organiza, ajuda, entrega.
Show results, not just duties. "Designed graphics for 3 campaigns" beats "Responsible for design."
"Dezeña gráfiku ba kampaña 3" di'ak liu hosi "Responsável ba dezeñu."
Check spelling, dates, phone number, and email address carefully.
Verifika ortografia, data, numeru telefone, no email ho kuidadu.
Keep formatting simple — no tables, columns, or fancy fonts that break when emailed.
Rai formatu simples — tabela ka koluna bele la'o sala.
One to two pages maximum. Be selective.
Pájina ida ka rua másimu. Hili ho kuidadu.
Employers often spend less than 30 seconds on a first read. Your name, current role, and top achievement should all be visible without scrolling.
Empregadór sira beibeik gasta menus hosi segundu 30 iha leitura primeiru. Ita-nia naran, kargu atual, no realizasaun boot tenke haree lalais.

I am a [role/field] professional with [X years] of experience in [key skill area]. I am known for [strength], and I am seeking a role where I can [goal]. My recent work includes [one-line achievement].

Hau mak profisionál [kargu/área] ho esperiénsia [tinan X] iha [área kbiit]. Hau mak ema ne'ebé [forsa], no hau buka kargu ne'ebé hau bele [objetivu]. Hau nia servisu resénte inklui [realizasaun ida ne'ebé badak].
A great cover letter does one thing: convince the reader you understand what they need and you can deliver it.
Cover letter di'ak ida halo buat ida: konvense ema ne'ebé lee katak ita komprende saida mak sira presiza no ita bele entrega.
Open with why you want this specific role, not just any job.
Hahu ho tamba sa ita hakarak kargu espesífiku ida-ne'e, la'ós de'it servisu deit.
Show you understand the organisation and its work.
Hatudu katak ita komprende organizasaun no sira-nia servisu.
Choose one or two examples that prove your key strengths.
Hili ezemplu ida ka rua ne'ebé prova ita-nia forsa.
Do not repeat your CV — add context, motivation, and personality.
Keta repete CV tomak — tau motivasaun, kontextu, no personalidade.
Keep it to one page. Every sentence must earn its place.
Rai ba pájina ida. Fraze ida-idak tenke hetan nia fatin.
Close with confidence: 'I would welcome the chance to discuss this further.'
Taka ho konfiansa: 'Hau sei kontente atu diskute ida-ne'e liután.'
Address the letter to a named person if you can find one. 'Dear Hiring Manager' is fine. 'To whom it may concern' feels like you did not try.
Hatete naran ema ida se bele hetan. 'Karo Responsável Rekrutamentu' di'ak. 'Ba ema ne'ebé preokupa' haree hanesan ita la ezersitu.

I am writing to apply for the [role title] position at [organisation]. I am drawn to this role because [specific reason related to the organisation's work]. In my current/previous role at [employer], I [key achievement that is relevant to this job].
Hau hakerek atu aplika ba pozisaun [títulu kargu] iha [organizasaun]. Hau interesa ba kargu ida-ne'e tanba [razaun espesífiku]. Iha hau-nia kargu antes iha [empregadór], hau [realizasaun relevante].
Interviews reward preparation. Candidates who practise out loud, prepare examples, and research the organisation consistently perform better.
Entrevista rekompensia preparasaun. Kandidatu ne'ebé prátika iha lian aas no prepara ezemplu sira konsistentemente halao di'ak liu.
Research the organisation — know their mission, recent work, and values.
Buka informasaun kona-ba organizasaun — hatene sira-nia misaun no servisu resénte.
Prepare three to five strong examples using the situation-action-result format.
Prepara ezemplu tolu to'o lima uza formatu situasaun-asaun-rezultadu.
Practise answering out loud, not just in your head.
Prátika resposta iha lian aas, la'ós de'it iha ita-nia ulun.
Prepare honest, confident answers to: 'Tell me about yourself' and 'What is your greatest weakness?'
Prepara resposta loloos ba: 'Konta kona-ba ita rasik' no 'Saida mak ita-nia frakeza boot?'
Arrive on time, dress professionally, and bring copies of your CV.
To'o iha tempu, hatais ho profisionalismu, no lori kópia CV.
Prepare two thoughtful questions to ask at the end.
Prepara pergunta rua ne'ebé matenek atu husu iha rohan.
Send a short thank-you message within 24 hours.
Haruka mensajen obrigadu badak iha oras 24.
The STAR method: describe the Situation, the Task, the Action you took, and the Result. This structure helps you give complete, impressive answers.
Metodu STAR: deskreve Situasaun, Tarefa, Asaun ne'ebé ita halo, no Rezultadu. Estrutura ida-ne'e ajuda ita fó resposta kompletu no forte.

Situation: [Brief context — where, when, what was happening]
Task: [What you were responsible for]
Action: [Specifically what YOU did — use 'I', not 'we']
Result: [What happened — include numbers or impact if possible]

Situasaun: [Kontextu badak — iha ne'ebé, bainhira, saida mak akontese]
Tarefa: [Saida mak ita responsável ba]
Asaun: [Espesifikamente saida mak ITA halo — uza 'Hau', la'ós 'Ami']
Resultadu: [Saida mak akontese — inklui numberu ka impaktu se bele]
Build a Portfolio or Capability Profile
In creative, communications, and project roles, showing your work is often more powerful than talking about it.
Iha kargu kriativu no komunikasaun, hatudu ita-nia servisu beibeik maka'as liu hosi ko'alia kona-ba nia.
Writers: articles, stories, reports, scripts, social media posts.
Eskrítor sira: artigu, istória, relatóriu, script, post sosial media.
Designers: posters, layouts, branding work, digital graphics.
Dezainer sira: poster, layout, branding, gráfiku dijitál.
Illustrators: character samples, page spreads, concept sketches.
Ilustradór sira: amostra karakter, pájina, concept sketch.
Photographers/videographers: curated, edited examples — quality over quantity.
Fotógrafu/videógrafu: ezemplu editadu ne'ebé hili — kualidade liu hosi kuantidade.
Admin and project staff: presentations, plans, reports, spreadsheets.
Pesoál admin/projetu: aprezentasaun, planu, relatóriu, spreadsheet.
Create a simple one-page PDF capability profile if you do not have a full portfolio yet.
Kria PDF simples kona-ba ita-nia kapasidade se seidauk iha portfolio boot.
A Google Drive folder shared via link is a practical, free option.
Pasta Google Drive ne'ebé fahe liu husi link mak opsaun prátiku no gratuitu.
Include a brief note with each piece of work explaining what you were asked to do, what you produced, and what the outcome was. Context makes work more impressive.
Tau nota badak ba serviçu ida-idak ne'ebé esplika saida mak ita hato'o, saida mak ita produz, no saida mak rezultadu nian. Kontextu halo servisu sai impresante liu.
Build Experience When No Jobs Are Listed
Harii Esperiénsia Bainhira Vaga La Iha
Most opportunities do not appear on job boards. Build experience, connections, and visibility before vacancies open.
Oportunidade barak la mosu iha plataforma vaga. Harii esperiénsia, relasaun, no visibilidade molok vaga mosu.
Volunteer for a community group, school, church, NGO, or local project.
Halo voluntáriu ba grupu komunidade, eskola, igreja, ONG, ka projetu lokal.
Offer to help with a short project to build real examples of your work.
Oferece ajuda ba projetu badak atu harii amostra servisu.
Contact organisations directly — ask if they accept CVs for future roles.
Kontaktu organizasaun sira diretamente — husu se sira simu CV ba oportunidade futura.
Join professional groups (online or local) where jobs and opportunities are shared.
Tama grupu profisionál (online ka lokal) ne'ebé fahe vaga.
Attend events, workshops, and community meetings to expand your network.
Partisipa iha eventu, workshop, no enkontru komunidade.
Create practice projects to demonstrate initiative and ability.
Kria projetu prátika atu hatudu iniciativa.
Document everything you do — it all becomes portfolio material.
Dokumenta saida mak ita halo — buat hotu bele sai material portfolio.
A personal email to the right person at an organisation — even when there is no advertised vacancy — can often lead to an interview. Keep it brief, professional, and specific.
Email pesoál ba ema di'ak iha organizasaun ida — maski la iha vaga anunsiadu — bele hamosu entrevista. Rai badak, profisionál, no espesífiku.

Subject: Expression of Interest — [Your Field/Role]

Dear [Name or Hiring Team],

I am [your name], a [role/field] professional based in [location]. I admire [organisation]'s work in [specific area] and am writing to enquire whether you have any upcoming opportunities in [your field].

I have experience in [key skill 1], [key skill 2], and [key skill 3]. I have attached my CV and would welcome the chance to speak further.

Thank you for your time.
[Your name]

Asuntu: Espresaun Interese — [Ita-nia Área/Kargu]

Karo [Naran ka Ekipa Rekrutamentu],

Hau mak [ita-nia naran], profisionál [kargu/área] bazeia iha [fatin]. Hau admira servisu [organizasaun] iha [área espesífiku] no hakerek atu kestiona se iha oportunidade iha [ita-nia área].

Hau iha esperiénsia iha [kbiit 1], [kbiit 2], no [kbiit 3]. Hau anexa hau-nia CV no sei kontente atu ko'alia liután.

Obrigadu ba ita-nia tempu.
[Ita-nia naran]

When competition is strong, small things matter. These habits separate strong candidates from the rest.
Bainhira kompetisaun boot, detalhu ki'ik sira mak importante. Hábitu sira-ne'e separa kandidatu sira forte hosi sira seluk.
Follow application instructions exactly — if they ask for a PDF, send a PDF.
Tuir instrusaun aplikasaun hotu ho loos — se sira husu PDF, haruka PDF.
Tailor your CV and cover letter to every single role.
Hadia CV no cover letter ba kargu ida-idak.
Include examples of relevant work wherever possible.
Inklui ezemplu servisu relevante iha fatin hotu.
Demonstrate reliability and professionalism in every interaction.
Hatudu katak ita bele fiar no profisionál iha interasaun hotu.
Communicate clearly and politely — emails, phone calls, and messages all count.
Ko'alia ho klaru no respeitu — email, telefone, no mensajen hotu-hotu importante.
Follow up respectfully if you have not heard back within two weeks.
Follow-up ho respeitu se la simu notísia iha semana rua.
Keep a spreadsheet of every application so you stay organised.
Uza spreadsheet atu rejista aplikasaun hotu.
Employers often remember candidates who send a brief, professional follow-up. It demonstrates genuine interest and good communication — two things every employer values.
Empregadór sira beibeik lembra kandidatu ne'ebé haruka follow-up profisionál badak. Nia hatudu interese loloos no komunikasaun di'ak — buat rua ne'ebé empregadór hotu valoriza.
